meta.title: Equal-total harmonic-spectrum benchmark for Scenario 2(M) and Scenario 3(M)
meta.assumption_note: This screen keeps the total harmonic current constant across Scenario 2(M) and Scenario 3(M). Scenario 2(M) distributes that total current across four AC-fed SST interfaces, while Scenario 3(M) injects the same total current through one centralized front end. The spectra are benchmark assumptions, not vendor spectra or IEEE 519 compliance cases.

spectra.name: low_order_dominant
spectra.note: Benchmark spectrum dominated by 5th and 7th current components.

spectra.name: balanced_filtered
spectra.note: Benchmark spectrum with the same total current spread evenly across the tested orders.

spectra.name: higher_order_filtered
spectra.note: Benchmark spectrum shifted toward higher orders to emulate stronger low-order filtering.

summary.interpretation: If Scenario 3(M) remains below Scenario 2(M) under equal-total harmonic injection, then the power-quality benefit is not just an artifact of injecting less total current. It also reflects the stronger centralized interconnection point.
